---
title: "Science & Innovation"
description: "Research and development spending — Canada compared to OECD peers"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, DATA_DATE,
)
```

## R&D Expenditure as % of GDP

Gross Domestic Expenditure on R&D (GERD) as a percentage of GDP is a standard measure of a nation's investment in research and development. This chart shows Canada alongside its OECD peers.

::: {.callout-note}
**Data lag:** R&D expenditure statistics are compiled nationally and reported to the OECD with a significant delay — typically 2–3 years. Countries report at different speeds, so the most recent year may not have full coverage. The OECD updates the MSTI database twice per year (March and September). The peer average line is only shown for years where at least half of peer countries have reported.
:::

Click any country name in the legend to toggle it on/off. Double-click to isolate one country.

```{python}
#| echo: false
df = pd.read_csv("../data/science/oecd_rd_expenditure.csv")

fig = go.Figure()

# Add peers first (grey, behind)
for code in sorted(df["country_code"].unique()):
    if code == HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY:
        continue
    subset = df[df["country_code"] == code]
    name = PEER_COUNTRIES.get(code, code)
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=subset["year"],
        y=subset["rd_pct_gdp"],
        name=name,
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(color=PEER_COLOR, width=PEER_WIDTH),
        opacity=0.5,
        hovertemplate=f"<b>{name}</b>: %{{y:.2f}}%<extra></extra>",
    ))

# Canada on top (red, bold)
can = df[df["country_code"] == HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=can["year"],
    y=can["rd_pct_gdp"],
    name="Canada",
    mode="lines+markers",
    line=dict(color=CANADA_COLOR, width=HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH),
    marker=dict(size=5),
    hovertemplate="<b>Canada</b>: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>",
))

# OECD average — only for years where at least half of peers report
min_countries = len(df["country_code"].unique()) // 2
year_counts = df.groupby("year")["country_code"].nunique()
valid_years = year_counts[year_counts >= min_countries].index
oecd_avg = (df[df["year"].isin(valid_years)]
            .groupby("year")["rd_pct_gdp"].mean().reset_index())
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=oecd_avg["year"],
    y=oecd_avg["rd_pct_gdp"],
    name="Peer Average",
    mode="lines",
    line=dict(color="#1f77b4", width=2, dash="dash"),
    hovertemplate="<b>Peer Average</b>: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>",
))

fig.update_layout(
    title="Gross Domestic Expenditure on R&D (GERD) as % of GDP",
    xaxis_title="Year",
    yaxis_title="R&D Expenditure (% of GDP)",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    xaxis=dict(
        gridcolor="#e0e0e0",
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=[
                dict(count=10, label="10Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=15, label="15Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="All"),
            ],
            x=0, xanchor="left", y=1.15,
        ),
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
    hovermode="x unified",
    legend=dict(
        orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.3,
        xanchor="center", x=0.5,
        itemclick="toggle", itemdoubleclick="toggleothers",
    ),
    margin=dict(b=140, t=80),
    annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Main Science and Technology Indicators (MSTI) | Data as of: {DATA_DATE}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.42,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
    )],
)
fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Latest Values: R&D Spending Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Bar chart — use latest year with data for most countries
year_counts = df.groupby("year")["country_code"].nunique()
latest_year = year_counts[year_counts >= 15].index.max()
latest = df[df["year"] == latest_year].copy()
latest["country_name"] = latest["country_code"].map(PEER_COUNTRIES)
latest = latest.sort_values("rd_pct_gdp", ascending=True)

colors = [CANADA_COLOR if c == "CAN" else PEER_COLOR for c in latest["country_code"]]

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=latest["rd_pct_gdp"],
    y=latest["country_name"],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=colors,
    hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.2f}%<extra></extra>",
))

fig.update_layout(
    title=f"R&D Expenditure as % of GDP ({latest_year})",
    xaxis_title="% of GDP",
    yaxis_title="",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
    showlegend=False,
    height=600,
    annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {DATA_DATE}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=1, y=-0.1,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
    )],
)
fig.show()
```

## Download Data

- [OECD R&D Expenditure (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_rd_expenditure.csv) — Gross Domestic Expenditure on R&D as % of GDP, 20 OECD peer countries, 2000–present
